summary: Iter9 validated (1) same-day non-collapse in Vibração 'Tabela de Dados' + Medições 'Tendência' modal and (2) new Cards/Lista view toggle on Máquinas (Vibração) and Termografia, with search working in both views. Backend regression: /api/measurements for ESF-0001/Motor 01 returns exactly 10 rows (1HA=3, 1HV=4, 2HV=3), all with the 'ordem' field. The 4 1HV rows include 2 same-day readings on 2026-06-15 (12:00 UTC = 09:00 BRT valor 9.2 and 18:30 UTC = 15:30 BRT valor 10.5). Frontend: Tabela de Dados renders 4 distinct date+time columns (15/04/2026 09:00, 15/05/2026 09:00, 15/06/2026 09:00, 15/06/2026 15:30) for ESF-0001/Motor 01 with 1HV values [3.1, 5.8, 9.2, 10.5] — same-day NOT collapsed. Medições tab: 1HV valor atual = 10.5 mm/s with A2 alarm; trend-modal renders 4 dots + 4 X-axis date/time labels including both 15/06 readings. Máquinas → Lista: correct 8 headers (TAG, Equipamento, Local, RPM, Pontos, Pico, Status, Diagnósticos); ESF-0001/Motor 01 row shows Pico '10.5 mm/s @ 1HV'; clicking row opens machine-modal; view-cards-btn/view-lista-btn toggle correctly and search filter works in both views. Termografia → Lista: 200 rows with correct headers (TAG, Equipamento, Local, Componente, Status, Últ. diag.); search 'QDE-229' filters to 26 rows in both lista and cards views; toggle works.
passed_backend: 1HV has 2 same-day readings on 2026-06-15 (12:00 UTC valor=9.2 alarme=A2 + 18:30 UTC valor=10.5 alarme=A2)
1HV latest reading (10.5) has alarme=A2 as expected for the 'valor atual' medição
1HA (3.1/5.8/9.2 for 1HV, 0.4/0.6/0.85 for 1HA, 1.2/1.5/2.0 for 2HV) all match seed spec
passed_frontend: /vibracao tab-tabela + filter ESF-0001/Motor 01: 4 distinct date+time columns (15/04/2026 09:00, 15/05/2026 09:00, 15/06/2026 09:00, 15/06/2026 15:30) — same-day NOT collapsed
1HV row values [3.1, 5.8, 9.2, 10.5] appear in the 4 columns in order
/vibracao tab-medicoes: 1HV row of ESF-0001/Motor 01 shows valor 10.5 mm/s + A2 alarm + data 15/06/2026
trend-btn on 1HV opens trend-modal with 4 recharts dots and 4 X-axis labels (visually verified: 15/04/2026 09:00, 15/05/2026 09:00, 15/06/2026 09:00, 15/06/2026 15:30)
view-lista-btn shows maquinas-lista table with headers [TAG, Equipamento, Local, RPM, Pontos, Pico, Status, Diagnósticos]
maquina-lista-row-'ESF-0001 / Motor 01': Pico='10.5 mm/s @ 1HV', clicking row opens machine-modal
view-cards-btn returns to cards view (lista removed from DOM)
Search input filters rows in both maquinas-lista and card views
Vibração card ESF-0001/Motor 01 now displays 'Pico: 10.5 mm/s @ 1HV' (updated from 9.2)
termo-view-lista shows termo-lista table with headers [TAG, Equipamento, Local, Componente, Status, Últ. diag.] and 200 rows
Search 'QDE-229' in lista filters to 26 rows (all with QDE-229 in TAG)
termo-view-cards returns to cards; search still filters to 26 cards

minor.issue: Legacy measurements imported before iter8 (e.g. BBA-0001 rows visible in the unfiltered Tabela de Dados) still lack the 'ordem' field in DB and get default sort behavior. Not blocking (frontend tolerates via `m.ordem || 0`) but recommended to backfill: db.measurements.update_many({ordem:{$exists:false}}, {$set:{ordem:0}}). Same as iter8.

issues: When 'Todas as máquinas' is selected, columns from mixed dates (15/04/2026 09:00 vs 02/07/2026 08:46) show many empty cells — expected pivot semantics, but the header row becomes very wide with 9+ columns of near-identical timestamps (02/07/2026 08:46 appears 5× because different machines were imported within the same minute). Consider deduplicating column headers by full timestamp equality only when the values truly differ, OR bucketing to the minute. Cosmetic only.

ESF-0001/Motor 01 shows Status='A1' but the 1HV latest measurement is alarme=A2 (10.5 mm/s). Status is driven by the latest diagnostic (A1) not the latest measurement alarm — this matches iter7 design ('status = last diag'), but it's counter-intuitive when the peak Pico cell shows an A2-worthy value. Consider surfacing 'measurement alarm' as a distinct badge or elevating the max(diag, measurement) alarm — behaviour identical in Cards view so no regression, just UX concern.
Persistent 'span cannot be a child of option' warning in console — visual-editor tooling injects <span> inside <option>. Dev-only, no functional impact.

critical_code_review_comments: Vibracao.js pivot col dedup uses `m.data` (full ISO timestamp) — correct for iter9 requirement. If two imports land at exactly the same ISO second they still collapse; not a concern for current seed but worth noting for the XLSX importer to preserve millisecond precision or append an ordinal suffix on ties.
Vibracao.js pointHistory sorts by (a.data||'').localeCompare(b.data||'') — ISO strings sort lexically = chronologically for UTC values. OK.

Vibracao.js line 341 `formatDateTime(c)` and Medições table line 313 still uses `formatDate(m.data)` (date only). Inconsistent granularity — same-day readings look identical in Medições but the trend modal shows time. Not a bug (Medições shows only the latest per point), but for symmetry consider `formatDateTime` in the 'Atualizado' column too.

Termografia.js line 15 useState('cards') is fine; termo-lista renders 200 rows without virtualization — acceptable at current scale but if the fleet grows to 5k+ termo points a react-virtual/tanstack-virtual pass would help. No action needed now.

Peak calc in Vibracao.js still unit-blind (compares mm/s vs g by raw valor) — iter7/iter8 comment carried forward. For ESF-0001/Motor 01 the unambiguous mm/s peak is 10.5 > 2.0 > 0.85g, so display is correct.
action_items: OPTIONAL (from iter8 carry-over): Backfill missing 'ordem' on legacy measurement docs.
OPTIONAL cosmetic: Consider bucketing/deduplicating pivot column headers on 'Todas as máquinas' view since same-minute imports produce visually duplicated timestamp columns.

OPTIONAL UX: Reconcile Status badge (last diag) vs Pico measurement alarm on maquinas-lista/cards — currently ESF-0001/Motor 01 shows A1 status while Pico A2 measurement (10.5) is present. Not a regression.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (6/6 iter9 flows: Tabela dedup, Medições valor+alarme, trend modal, maquinas lista+toggle+search, termografia lista+toggle+search, machine-modal open from lista)

seed_data_creation: None — read-only validation against existing seed (ESF-0001/Motor 01 with 4×1HV, 3×1HA, 3×2HV).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter9 validated same-day non-collapse (Vibracao.js pivot uses m.data ISO instead of formatDate). formatDateTime renders America/Sao_Paulo local time so 12:00Z→09:00 and 18:30Z→15:30 as expected. Cards/Lista toggles: view-cards-btn/view-lista-btn on /vibracao Máquinas tab, termo-view-cards/termo-view-lista on /termografia. Lista row testids: maquina-lista-row-{tag} (contains slashes/spaces — use document.querySelector with double quotes in evaluate). Regression: /app/backend/tests/test_iter9_same_day_measurements.py. Note: after clearing search input, add a small wait (400ms) before clicking view toggle buttons — otherwise the first click can be intercepted by React re-render (observed once in this iteration, then reliable).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. The transient click-race on view-cards-btn was resolved by adding 400ms wait between search fill('') and next click. Root cause: React state batching between input onChange and button onClick when both target the same parent component.
